description: next/ v2.0 — Level-3 MOM OperationsRequest = the PLAN/target of manufacturing (B2MML OperationsRequest / ISA-95 Part 4). Opposite side to the OperationsResponse (the actuals). A Level-4 SalesOrder (SMProfile-CustomerOrder) TRIGGERS this L3 OperationsRequest; EXECUTED_AT a Machine (≡ EquipmentUnit level, consolidation 2026-07-15). v2.0: the order INSTANTIATES an OperationsDefinition (routing master) and decomposes per ProcessSegment into SegmentRequirements (HAS_SEGMENT_REQUIREMENT); temporal subdivisions (FO index) as Workorder (HAS_WORKORDER). Plan-vs-actual (qty_shortfall, late_delivery) is Cross-Source (it-evaluator), not a single-entity constraints block.

attributes.name: production_order_no
attributes.description: Join key — identity of the OperationsRequest. The OperationsResponse reports its actuals back via the same key (RESPONDS_TO).

attributes.name: article_ref
attributes.description: Article to be manufactured (article_no, join key to Article).

attributes.name: machine_ref
attributes.description: Planned executing machine / line (machine_no). EXECUTED_AT Machine (machine_id).

attributes.name: qty_planned
attributes.description: Planned piece count (target). Counterpart to OperationsResponse.quantity_produced. Driver of the qty_shortfall rule.

attributes.name: planned_end_time
attributes.description: Planned completion date (target). Counterpart to actual_end_time. Driver of the late_delivery rule.

attributes.name: material_ready_at
attributes.description: Time from which material is available — material-ready constraint.

attributes.name: customer_order_qty
attributes.description: Customer order quantity of the triggering order (denormalized).

attributes.name: customer_due_date
attributes.description: Customer delivery date of the triggering order (denormalized).

attributes.name: status
attributes.description: Plan status of the OperationsRequest, as it reaches THIS profile through OUR source binding. VOCABULARY MEASURED 2026-07-12 against the full population of sim-v5 PROD (.154), not a sample and not the mapper's source code: the ERP contains 47.513 orders carrying exactly FIVE raw statuses — CANCELLED 21.676, INVOICED 19.827, IN_PRODUCTION 4.305, WAITING_PARTS 1.687, ON_HOLD 18. sources/rest/erp-production-orders.json binds /api/orders?exclude_status=CANCELLED,INVOICED,SHIPPED, so CANCELLED (-> storniert) and INVOICED (-> abgeschlossen) never arrive: 6.010 of 47.513 orders reach us, and the projection maps them onto exactly TWO canonical values — in_arbeit 4.305 (71,63 %) and freigegeben 1.705 (28,37 %, = WAITING_PARTS + ON_HOLD). THIS ENUM IS THE COMPLETE IMAGE, not a lower bound: the raw states that would be needed to produce any other value DO NOT EXIST IN THE ERP AT ALL. 'offen' requires raw DRAFT|PROPOSED|PLANNED and 'fertig' requires PRODUCED|COMPLETED — there is not ONE such row among all 47.513 (0,00 %); 'abgeschlossen' is reachable only from INVOICED, which our source excludes. Two earlier attempts got this wrong in opposite directions and BOTH were merged into this file's history: capt-qty-retire-shortfall measured the ENDPOINT and kept storniert+abgeschlossen (our own filter removes them); feat/ci-vocabulary-gate measured the PROJECTION's source code and kept offen+fertig+abgeschlossen (the raw states those branches need never occur, and its probe's 'fertig 1' is not reproducible — the true count is 0). The lesson is in the method: an enum must be measured where the data ENTERS US — endpoint AND source filter AND population — not at any one of the three. CONSEQUENCE: any `when` guard keying on 'offen' is structurally unsatisfiable and is a sleeping watchdog (this is why profiles/erp/customer-order.json#order_overdue is retired, and why ci/lint-vocabulary.mjs — which would have PASSED that guard against the fictional enum above — is only as honest as the enum it checks). Extend this enum ONLY against a fresh measurement of the full population.

attributes.name: updated_at
attributes.description: Last change. Server-managed and volatile on every read (sim-v5 returns a fresh value per GET), so promotion:never — kept in the snapshot but excluded from the change-trigger and never published on its own.

relationships.description: Consolidation 2026-07-15: Machine ≡ EquipmentUnit level, keyed machine_id — matches what the ERP source actually delivers (machine ids).
v2.0 — Inverse of SegmentRequirement.PART_OF: the target segments per ProcessSegment.
v2.0 — Inverse of Workorder.PART_OF: temporal subdivisions (FO index).

isa95.note: Level-3 MOM OperationsRequest (B2MML) = PLAN/target. v2.0: the earlier flat SegmentRequirement.MaterialRequirement projection (propertyMap) is now modeled out as a dedicated SMProfile-SegmentRequirement per ProcessSegment (HAS_SEGMENT_REQUIREMENT); the order INSTANTIATES an OperationsDefinition and can be subdivided via Workorder (FO index). article_ref/machine_ref remain a denormalized header shortcut. Plan-vs-actual = Cross-Source reconcile, not a constraints block. QUANTITY: qty_planned is optional and is omitted for continuous bulk/length-based goods (e.g. continuous web/strand goods, sold by length) — then only times/status drive the PLAN.
